example_message: scanf() without field width limits can crash with huge input data.
Either the condition 'i<64-1' is redundant or the array 'src[14]' is accessed at index 62, which is out of bounds.
If memory allocation fails, then there is a possible null pointer dereference: buffer
Returning/dereferencing 'buf' after it is deallocated / released
Dereferencing 'buffer' after it is deallocated / released
